dominColor.seq: rosybrown:2 darkslategray:1 gray:1 darkgray:0 silver:0 black:0 sienna:0 lightsalmon:0
darkgray:4 silver:2 firebrick:0 lightsteelblue:0 darkslateblue:0 midnightblue:0 darkslategray:0 maroon:0 brown:0

text.transcription: ICI REPOSENT DES COMBANTTANTS FRANÇAIS/HIER RUSTEN FRANSE STRIJDERS/HIER RUHEN FRANZÖSISCHE SOLDATEN/HERE REST FRENCH SOLIDIERS

description.div: A site marker, indicating the opening and closing time of the cemetery.
Remarkable is the reference to wind, probably as a disclaimer in case a visitor to the cemetery is hit by a falling branch or a falling monument.
Interesting is also the use of the Dutch word \'rukwind\', different from \'rugwind\'. \'rukken\' is a verb translatable as \'pull\' or \'tug\', while \'rug\' refers to \'the back of a person\'.

dominColor.seq: darkslategray:3 gray:1 silver:1 black:1 darkgray:0 dimgray:0

text.transcription: OPENINGSUREN/WESTERBEGRAAFPLAATS/Van april tot en meet 30 september/van 7 tot 20 uur/Van oktober tot en met 31 maart/van 8 tot 17.30 uur/Toegang verboden bij hevig rukwinden (vanaf code oranje)

normalization.div: Van april tot en meet 30 september van 7 tot 20 uur.
Van oktober tot en met 31 maart van 8 tot 17.30 uur.
Toegang verboden bij hevig rukwinden (vanaf code oranje).

trans.div: OPENING HOURS for the WESTERN CEMETERY: From April to September 30, from 7 a.m. to 8 p.m..From October to March 31, from 8 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. The access is prohibited during strong winds, starting from code orange.

dominColor.seq: silver:4 darkslategray:2 lightslategray:1 dimgray:0 darkolivegreen:0 gainsboro:0 bisque:0
darkslategray:3 dimgray:1 lavender:0 lightcyan:0 lightslategray:0 black:0 lightsteelblue:0

names.name: Site marker of the "French Military Cemetery"
marqueur de site du "Cimétiere Militaire Français"

dominColor.seq: rosybrown:3 gray:3 darkgray:1 silver:0 tan:0
dimgray:6 lightgray:2 silver:0 rosybrown:0 darkslategray:0 darkgray:0

text.transcription: SACRARIO MILITARE/IN MEMORIAM/TOMBE DI COMBATTENTI ITALIANI/1914-1918/UNUCI-BELGIO

dominColor.seq: rosybrown:2 gray:1 darkslategray:1 darkgray:1 dimgray:0 silver:0 olivedrab:0 beige:0
darkslategray:3 gray:1 dimgray:1 darkgray:0 darkseagreen:0
darkgray:3 darkslategray:3 silver:2 gainsboro:0 gray:0 brown:0 rosybrown:0
darkslategray:4 dimgray:0 darkgray:0 black:0 darkolivegreen:0
rosybrown:2 darkgray:2 gray:1 silver:1 tan:0 dimgray:0 gainsboro:0

description.div: The image shows a few monuments in the older part of the Westerbegraafplaats.

dominColor.seq: darkslategray:2 dimgray:0 darkgray:0 darkseagreen:0 silver:0 linen:0

description.div: Westerbegraafplaats means literally the Western Burial Ground, located, as the name indicates, to the west of the city center. The Westerbegraafplaats is a large and diversified cemetery, having, among others, sections dedicated to war graves of different nations.

Ghent is the capital and the biggest city of the province East Flanders. Located at the confluence of the Rivers Scheldt and Leie. It is a port and university town and the third largest city of Belgium.

East Flanders is a province of Belgium, located to the east of the province West Flanders and the south of the Dutch provinde Zeeland. The capital and biggest city of East Flanders is Ghent. The province is mainly Dutch-speeking.

darkgray:6 darkkhaki:0 darkseagreen:0 darkslategray:0 tan:0
darkolivegreen:2 gray:1 darkgray:1 dimgray:1 silver:0

description.div: A wooden cross, uninscribed. The grave is covered by an uninscribed slate. The cross and the slate seem to have been applied recently to a grave that is older. The size of the grave might hint to the grave of a child. It might be worth to revisit the tomb to find out whether the grave markers are permanently uninscribed, of whether the photo of 2013 represents an itermediate state in the creation of a grave.

description.div: A black and white photo of Antoinetteke Coolean in her first year of life. The photo is printed on an oval ceramic support nicely surround by two golden circles. The two screws that hold the photo on the tombstone are rusting but have been obviously replaced a number of times.

ocr.orig: LIGT BEGRAY/DE EERW HIS/VAN DER HALLEA/PASTOOR/TE OPHOVEN/VAN 1871/ALDAAR GESTORE/DE S FEBR. 19/PARZIMONTA/A HASSELT

description.div: Ophoven is a community in the north of Kinrooi.
Kinrooi is a settlement in the arrondissement Maaseik, Belgium. It has the administrative status of a municipality.
Kinrooi is a municipality in the arrondissement Maaseik, Belgium.
Limburg, or Belgian Limburg, is one of the five Dutch-speaking provinces of the region of Flanders. Part of Limburg is the Dutch-speaking enclave Voeren in the French-speaking province Liège.

Not much fieldwork has been done in Limburg so far. Of particular interest for fieldwork would be the municipality of Voeren in the French-speaking province Liège with respect to the visibility of Dutch and French respectively, compared to other municipalities of Belgian Limburg and Liège.

Belgium, situated at the crossroads of Western Europe, presents a fascinating linguistic mosaic. The northern region (Flanders) is predominantly Dutch-speaking, while the southern region (Wallonia) is primarily French-speaking, with a small German-speaking community nestled along the eastern border with Germany. Brussels, the capital, presents an intriguing linguistic anomaly: despite being geographically surrounded by Dutch-speaking territories, it functions largely as a French-speaking enclave —a linguistic island shaped by the historical prestige French maintained throughout the region for centuries.

Urban centers like Brussels have become important sites for linguistic landscape research, where scholars analyze public written language on signs, advertisements, and official notices to understand language distribution and power dynamics. However, these conventional linguistic landscape methodologies primarily capture formal, public language usage while often overlooking private linguistic practices.

The Thakbong project expands traditional linguistic landscape research by examining language choices in burial sites and prayer spaces—intimate domains where cultural identity, personal history, and communal belonging intersect. By documenting linguistic practices in these sacred spaces, the project reveals dimensions of language use that remain invisible in standard public signage studies, offering deeper insights into authentic community language practices and heritage preservation. This conceptual link between the Thakbong project and linguistic research in the paradigm of "Linguistic Landscapes" has first been made by Jimmy Huang in his intervention on the first DRGT (not DRGPA) conference in 2011 at the Medical University of Taibei.
Belgium has been one of the founding members of the European Community and is, since then, its administrative center.
